title: HistoryCompressorOutput — History Compressor Structured Output
description: Future [FUTURE-ONLY] schema for the structured output produced by a model-assisted History Compressor. This schema is not used by any MVP pipeline module, CLI command, fixture, or harness test. Canonical source: docs/13_MODEL_ASSISTED_CONTEXT_PLANNING.md §10 'History Compression and Summary Safety'. No field from this schema may be added to any MVP schema without a separate explicit schema decision pass. additionalProperties is false — no undocumented fields are accepted.
$comment: Phase P2 of Model-Assisted Harness Scoping (docs/22). Created under schemas/future/ to preserve MVP Non-Interference Guarantee (docs/13 §2). The 11 required state extraction categories map to array-of-objects fields (structured state extractor per docs/13 §10, not paragraph summarizer). currentTaskState is a single object (only one active task state per session). summaryTrace implements the 3 trace categories defined in docs/13 §10 (included/omitted/uncertain). compressorVersion and compressorTraceId follow the auditing pattern established in analyzer-output.schema.json (P1).

StateItem.title: Structured state item
StateItem.description: A single extracted state item produced by the history compressor. 'content' carries the core extracted text. 'notes' carries optional reasoning, source reference, or lifecycle metadata (e.g. why this item was retained, which turn it originated from).

content.description: The extracted state content. Required. Must be non-empty.

notes.description: Optional metadata: source reference, lifecycle note, reasoning for retention. Null or absent if not applicable.

compressorVersion.description: Identifier of the compressor model or version that produced this output. For audit and reproducibility. Not a schema version. Required. Follows the same auditing convention as analyzerVersion in analyzer-output.schema.json.

compressorTraceId.description: Unique ID linking this HistoryCompressorOutput to its trace entry in trace.json. Required for full traceability. Follows the same auditing convention as analyzerTraceId in analyzer-output.schema.json.

currentTaskState.description: The active task state at the time of compression — active task, current goal, and blockers. Defined as a single structured object (not an array) because there is only one active task state per session at any point. Canonical source: docs/13 §10 'Current task state: Active task, current goal, blockers'. Protected from compression per docs/13 §10 'Protected from Compression' table.

activeTask.description: Description of the active task the session is currently working on. Required.

currentGoal.description: The current goal within the active task. Optional — may be absent if not clearly established.

blockers.description: Known blockers currently preventing task progress. May be empty if no blockers are active.

acceptedDecisions.description: Decisions accepted during the session. Protected from compression per docs/13 §10 — must never be omitted by a future compressor. Prevents decision re-litigation. Canonical source: docs/13 §10 'Accepted decisions: Decisions accepted during the session'.

openIssues.description: Unresolved problems identified during the session but not yet addressed. Canonical source: docs/13 §10 'Open issues: Unresolved problems identified but not yet addressed'.

openCommitments.description: Promises, agreements, and pending deliverables made during the session. Protected from compression per docs/13 §10 — dropAllowed: false in MVP (docs/04 §7.6). Canonical source: docs/13 §10 'Open commitments: Promises, agreements, pending deliverables'.

userConstraints.description: User-stated requirements and preferences established during the session. Relates to the MVP userConstraints input (docs/06 §2.8) but is the compressor's structured extract, not the raw input. Canonical source: docs/13 §10 'User constraints: User-stated requirements and preferences'.

importantFilesPaths.description: Files, directories, and paths referenced as important during the session. 'content' should be the path or file reference. 'notes' may carry context about why this file/path is relevant. Canonical source: docs/13 §10 'Important files/paths: Files, directories, and paths referenced in the session'.

failedAttempts.description: Approaches tried and rejected during the session, with reasons. 'content' should describe the attempted approach. 'notes' should carry the reason for rejection. Critical for preventing re-attempting discarded solutions. Canonical source: docs/13 §10 'Failed attempts: Approaches tried and rejected, with reasons'.

warnings.description: Active warnings and risk flags from the session. Canonical source: docs/13 §10 'Warnings: Active warnings and risk flags'.

antiRegressionRules.description: Hard lessons from the session (see docs/13 §13 'Anti-Regression Memory Lane'). Protected from compression per docs/13 §10 — prevents re-learning costly mistakes. 'content' carries the rule. 'notes' may carry category, severity, source reference, or applicability scope (per docs/13 §13 rule structure). Canonical source: docs/13 §10 'Anti-regression rules: Hard lessons from the session'.

recentRelevantTurns.description: A configurable window of recent raw conversation turns retained alongside the structured summary. 'content' carries the raw turn text. 'notes' may carry turn index or timestamp. Relates to the MVP recent_raw_turns lane (docs/04 §7.6). Canonical source: docs/13 §10 'Recent relevant turns: A configurable window of recent raw conversation turns'.

durableFacts.description: Long-lived factual context established during the session. Relates to the MVP durable_facts lane (docs/04 §7.6) and the durable constraints category (dropAllowed: false). Protected from compression per docs/13 §10. Canonical source: docs/13 §10 'Durable facts: Long-lived factual context established in the session'.

summaryTrace.description: Trace output documenting what the compressor retained, omitted, and was uncertain about. Required for debugging: reveals whether a main model error was caused by the summary (omitted critical information) vs. the selector vs. prompt assembly. All three arrays are required (may be empty). Canonical source: docs/13 §10 'Summary Trace'.

included.description: State categories and items retained in the summary. Canonical source: docs/13 §10 Summary Trace table — 'included: State categories and items retained in the summary'.

omitted.description: Items deliberately excluded by the compressor (e.g. obsolete rejected drafts, duplicate reports). Canonical source: docs/13 §10 Summary Trace table — 'omitted: Items deliberately excluded'.

uncertain.description: Items the compressor was not confident about retaining or omitting. Essential for fail-open auditability. Canonical source: docs/13 §10 Summary Trace table — 'uncertain: Items the compressor was not confident about retaining or omitting'.
